growth_records.strain: Ca. Nitrososphaera gargensis (AOA, Thaumarchaeota group I.1b)

up.method: Michaelis-Menten fit; Km(app)=3.3-6.8 uM total ammonium (range for the two Nitrososphaera isolates, n=4 each; ~410-703 nM NH3); protein-normalized Vmax not given in text (Lineweaver-Burk culture Vmax 28.5 uM h-1, not biomass-normalized)

sec.method: AOA: incomplete oxidizer, product is nitrite; rate not protein-normalized in text

yields.Yxs: 298.4 ug protein per mol NH3 oxidized (s.d. 24.2, n=4)

growth_records.cite: Kits et al. Kinetic analysis of a complete nitrifier reveals an oligotrophic lifestyle. Nature (2017). DOI:10.1038/nature23679. [PMC5600814]
growth_records.snippet: The mean Km(app) values of the two group I.1b AOA isolates of the genus Nitrososphaera (3.3 to 6.8 uM total ammonium, equivalent to ~410 to 703 nM NH3, n = 4 each) ... that of N. gargensis was 298.4 ug protein per mol NH3 (s.d. 24.2, n = 4)
growth_records.notes: Km(app, total ammonium)=3.3-6.8 uM (range covers both Nitrososphaera isolates gargensis and viennensis, n=4 each). Molar growth yield 298.4 ug protein/mol NH3 (reported). Lineweaver-Burk normalized culture Vmax for gargensis = 28.5 uM h-1 but NOT biomass/protein-normalized, so not a usable specific uptake rate; left null. AO medium composition referenced elsewhere -> not enumerated. Optimum temperature 46 C. No growth rate/doubling time measured. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]
